notes: Can confirm that it works out of the box. Adding "-CustomResolution:enabled -r1920x1080x32 -f -intro:off" is a nice touch however due to the modified resolution, it starts to lag especially when zooming in and out. This also happens on windows, so the game works incredibly well in proton .
title: SimCity™ 4 Deluxe Edition

notes: [PERFECT] On par with the windows one. Nothing to add nothing to remove everything is perfect.  || Launch options: -intro:off -w -r1600x900x32 -CustomResolution:enabled -CPUPriority:low -CPUCount:2 ||
title: SimCity™ 4 Deluxe Edition

notes:
Zero problems to report even with modding and ultrawide resolution.
Ran with the launch options: "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 -intro:off -f -r2560x1080x32 -CustomResolution:enabled -CPUPriority:high -CPUCount:2"

notes: In larger cities it tends to lag a bit but other than that it seems to run completely fine.
title: SimCity™ 4 Deluxe Edition

notes: Working perfectly out of the box.
title: SimCity™ 4 Deluxe Edition

notes: I had no issues with crashes or glitches while playing the game.
title: SimCity™ 4 Deluxe Edition

notes: Runs perfectly with no crashes that I've encountered. Lags slightly in large cities, but it always did running natively, too. Works great at ridiculous resolutions the game designers never predicted using the launch options "-CustomResolution:enabled -r3440x1440x32" (replace 3440x1440 with your own resolution). Add "-intro:off" to skip the long intro. I tried lowering the CPU count as others have suggested, but that made performance worse so I left it at the default. Works with mods, but they'll need to be installed in ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/24780/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/SimCity 4
title: SimCity™ 4 Deluxe Edition
